rulings.Case Title: T&C PROPERTIES, LLC VS. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: St. James Property Co., Llcs Notice Of Motion To Vacate, Or In The Alternative To Correct, Final Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 22, 2023 line 1. ST. JAMES PROPERTY CO., LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO VACATE, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO CORRECT, FINAL ARBITRATION AWARD is DENIED. Respondent to make a motion for attorney's fees before the entry of final judgment. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DAWUD DIRBAS VS. OLTON LAWRENCE RENSCH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 22, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT OLTON RENSCH'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND is DENIED. Moving party fails to carry their burden under CCP 473(b). Summons and Complaint served personally on December 19, 2022. Default entered over 20 days later. Having received a negative response from legal assistance agencies regarding representation of commercial tenants "immediately" after having been served, Defendant had sufficient time to respond before the entry of default as urged both in the Summons and in the Notice to Defendant. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
